message:
fix(sdk): thread globalObject through Next.js and React runtime diagnostics

Next.js adapter (packages/next/src/index.ts):
- detectNextRuntimeSupport now accepts an optional globalObject
  parameter and forwards it to detectBrowserRuntimeSupport. This
  allows server-side rendering (SSR) and test environments to inject
  a mock global scope for accurate capability detection instead of
  falling back to the ambient global, which may be Node's globalThis
  rather than a browser-like scope.
- Extract nextClientBrowserRuntimeOptions() helper that maps
  NextClientBootstrapOptions → BrowserRuntimeOptions, forwarding
  globalObject, preferredLane, healthPolicy, healthScopeKey, and now.
  Previously createBrowserRuntime received only wasmInput,
  consumerVersion, and eagerInit — the rest were silently dropped,
  causing the browser runtime to ignore the caller's lane preference,
  health policy, and custom clock.
- NextClientBootstrapAdapter.initializeRuntime now passes
  this.options.globalObject into detectNextRuntimeSupport and uses
  nextClientBrowserRuntimeOptions instead of inline object literal.

React adapter (packages/react/src/index.ts):
- detectReactRuntimeSupport now accepts an optional globalObject
  and forwards it to detectBrowserRuntimeSupport.
- ReactRuntimeProvider passes runtimeOptions?.globalObject into both
  the initial useState callback and the useEffect re-detection,
  ensuring diagnostics reflect the injected scope (if any) rather
  than the ambient global.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
